lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
While the Emperor Theodosius is engaged in Military Preparations against Maximus, his Son Honorius is born. He then proceeds to Milan in Order to encounter the Usurper.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: While the Emperor Theodosius is engaged in Military Preparations against Maximus, his Son Honorius is born. He then proceeds to Milan in Order to encounter the Usurper.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the Emperor Theodosius was filled with great solicitude, and levied a powerful army against the usurper, fearing lest he should meditate the assassination of the young Valentinian also.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: While engaged in this preparation, an embassy arrived from the Persians, requesting peace from the emperor.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Then also the empress Flaccilla bore him a son named Honorius, on the 9th of September, in the consulate of Richomelius and Clearchus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Under the same consulate, and a little previously, Agelius bishop of the Novatians died.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: In the year following, wherein Arcadius Augustus bore his first consulate in conjunction with Baudon, Timothy bishop of Alexandria died, and was succeeded in the episcopate by Theophilus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: About a year after this, Demophilus the Arian prelate having departed this life, the Arians sent for Marinus a leader of their own heresy out of Thrace, to whom they entrusted the bishopric: but Marinus did not long occupy that position, for under him that sect was divided into two parties, as we shall hereafter explain; for they invited Dorotheus to come to them from Antioch in Syria, and constituted him their bishop.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Meanwhile the emperor Theodosius proceeded to the war against Maximus, leaving his son Arcadius with imperial authority at Constantinople.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Accordingly arriving at Thessalonica he found Valentinian and those about him in great anxiety, because through compulsion they had acknowledged the usurper as emperor.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Theodosius, however, gave no expression to his sentiments in public; he neither rejected nor admitted the embassy of Maximus: but unable to endure tyrannical domination over the Roman empire, under the assumption of an imperial name, he hastily mustered his forces and advanced to Milan, whither the usurper had already gone.
